description.en: This ontology models the European catalogue of high-risk medical devices and in vitro diagnostic devices under the regulatory framework of the MDR (EU 2017/745) and IVDR (EU 2017/746) regulations.

comment.en: Security patch 3.0.1 mandatory deployment disabling unrestricted web interface access. Network segmentation guidance issued to hospital IT departments. Penetration test report provided to competent authorities.
Enhanced surveillance programme: annual DEXA scan and serum particle monitoring for patients with affected tibial inserts. Patients contacted individually.
Enhanced deployment training and updated IFU for Portico valve implantation technique. New implantation checklist mandatory for all TAVI centres.

Enhanced calibration protocol issued. Firmware update v1.8.5 mandatory improving temperature compensation algorithm. Patients instructed to perform confirmatory finger-stick at glucose readings <4.5 or >13.9 mmol/L.
A FSCA involving correction or modification of the device at its current location.
The European regulatory framework applicable to a medical device.
The risk classification assigned to a medical device according to MDR or IVDR regulations.
In Vitro Diagnostic Regulation EU 2017/746 applicable to IVD devices.
SYNERGY stent fracture detected on angiography 18 months post-implant causing in-stent restenosis.
Plum 360 infusion pump delivered 10x heparin dose due to software decimal point error in drug library version 4.2.1.
OPTIGA CGM sensor reported glucose 3.8 mmol/L while laboratory reference showed 14.2 mmol/L. Patient delayed insulin administration.
Portico valve showed paravalvular leak grade III due to partial migration 2 weeks post-TAVI procedure.
Micra AV pacemaker reached elective replacement indicator 14 months earlier than expected longevity specification.
VIDAS HIV DUO Ultra failed to detect HIV-1 group O infection in seroconversion panel. Patient missed early treatment window.
cobas SARS-CoV-2 assay failed to detect Omicron BA.2.86 subvariant in 7 samples confirmed positive by sequencing. Lot-specific issue.
Patient implanted with HeartMate 3 LVAD developed ischemic stroke 6 months post-implant. Device thrombus confirmed by echocardiography.
cobas e 801 thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH) assay reported false positive results (>10 mIU/L) in 12 euthyroid patients due to biotin interference.
ATERA ventilator software version 2.1.3 delivered FiO2 6% lower than set value for patients in ARDS protocol. 3 patients affected.
MAGNETOM Vida MRI caused localised skin burn (1st degree) at anterior electrode lead in patient with DBS system not declared prior to scan.
Viva CRT-D delivered inappropriate ICD therapy during MRI scan despite MRI-conditional programming. Patient experienced pain and syncope.
TRITON hip patient presented with elevated serum cobalt (18 ppb). Adverse local tissue reaction (ALTR) confirmed by MRI.
Persona knee tibial insert showing accelerated polyethylene wear with metallosis confirmed at 3-year revision surgery.
5008S hemodialysis machine web interface exposed to hospital internal network. Unauthorised session accessed alarm configuration.
Iluvien implant required surgical removal due to device-related endophthalmitis confirmed by vitreous culture.
ACUSON Sequoia ultrasound system froze mid-examination. Emergency shutdown required; patient examination incomplete.
ACUITY X4 lead dislodgement to right ventricle requiring fluoroscopy-guided repositioning.
HeartMate 3 patient developed severe haemolysis (LDH >2000 U/L) associated with outflow graft kinking confirmed by CT.
An incident that directly or indirectly led to serious injury or death of a patient.
Metallic implant causing MRI image artifacts compromising diagnostics
External EMI affecting pacemaker/ICD sensing and pacing functions
Risk type related to electromagnetic or device interference.
An action taken to reduce a risk of death or serious deterioration in health associated with a device in use.
Battery end-of-life earlier than specified, risking therapy interruption
Software bug causing incorrect dosing, alarming, or therapy delivery
Complete loss of electrical function leading to device shutdown
Structural failure of mechanical components under physiological load
Risk category related to device failure.
Continuous glucose monitor for patients with diabetes
An IVD device intended to be used by lay persons in a home environment.
Tivoli II cochlear implant failed to respond to initial activation programming. Sound processor replacement resolved the issue.
An incident that could have led to patient harm but did not due to fortunate circumstances.
Software bug causing incorrect dosing, alarming, or therapy delivery
Risk type related to software malfunction or erroneous output.
Metallic ion release from corrosion causing local or systemic toxicity
Particulate debris from bearing surfaces causing adverse tissue reaction
Risk type related to material degradation, wear debris or toxicity.
Competent authority: Läkemedelsverket (MPA)
Competent authority: Rijksinstituut voor Volksgezondheid en Milieu (RIVM)
Competent authority: Agence nationale de sécurité du médicament (ANSM)

labels.en: Agence nationale de sécurité du médicament (ANSM)

comment.en: Competent authority: Swissmedic
Competent authority: Agence fédérale des médicaments et des produits de santé (AFMPS)

labels.en: Agence fédérale des médicaments et des produits de santé (AFMPS)

comment.en: Competent authority: Health Products Regulatory Authority (HPRA)
Competent authority: Bundesinstitut für Arzneimittel und Medizinprodukte (BfArM)
Competent authority: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA)

labels.en: Medicines and Healthcare products Regulatory Agency (MHRA)

comment.en: The national authority responsible for supervising medical device manufacturers in a given country.
Any malfunction or deterioration in the characteristics of a device that has led or could lead to patient harm.
Unintended ionising radiation exposure exceeding therapeutic dose
Risk type related to unintended ionising radiation exposure.
MDR risk class IIa - medium risk.
MDR risk class IIb - medium-high risk.
Metallic ion release from corrosion causing local or systemic toxicity
Unintended ionising radiation exposure exceeding therapeutic dose
Thrombus formation on device surface causing embolic events
Mechanical destruction of red blood cells by pump mechanism
Device-related infection at implantation site or systemic sepsis
Bacterial biofilm accumulation on implant surface resistant to antibiotics
Particulate debris from bearing surfaces causing adverse tissue reaction
Risk category related to direct patient harm.
Ventricular assist device for end-stage heart failure
Drug-eluting coronary stent
An active device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
An incident involving a device classified as Class III, inferred by SWRL rule
A medical device as defined under MDR (EU 2017/745) or IVDR (EU 2017/746) regulations.
Reagent lot contamination leading to systematic diagnostic errors
Risk category related to product quality issues.
Exploitable software vulnerability enabling unauthorized device access
Risk type related to cybersecurity vulnerabilities in active devices.
Exploitable software vulnerability enabling unauthorized device access
Risk category related to device safety vulnerabilities.
MDR risk class I - lowest risk.
IVDR risk class C - medium-high risk.
Manufacturer: GE HealthCare Technologies
Manufacturer: Zimmer Biomet Holdings
Manufacturer: LivaNova PLC
Manufacturer: Roche Diagnostics GmbH
Manufacturer: Boston Scientific Corporation
Manufacturer: Stryker Corporation
Manufacturer: Biotronik SE & Co. KG

Manufacturer: Nipro Corporation
Manufacturer: Abbott Medical Devices
Manufacturer: Siemens Healthineers AG
Manufacturer: Hologic Inc.
Manufacturer: Philips Medical Systems
Manufacturer: bioMérieux SA
A natural or legal person responsible for the design and manufacture of a medical device.
IVDR risk class B - medium risk.
Non-invasive prenatal testing (NIPT) for aneuploidies
Automated immunoassay analyzer for clinical laboratory
Molecular diagnostic test for SARS-CoV-2 detection
Combined HIV Ag/Ab detection assay
Urine-based prostate cancer diagnosis assay
A device used for examination of specimens derived from the human body.
IVDR risk class D - highest risk.
IVDR risk class A - lowest risk.
Urgent safety recall due to elevated metal ion levels. Revision surgery recommended for patients with serum cobalt >7 ppb or symptomatic ALTR. Replacement implants provided.
Voluntary recall of affected Micra AV lots due to premature battery depletion. Replacement units provided. 97% of affected devices successfully replaced.
Urgent recall and remote software update. Drug library version 4.3.0 deployed correcting decimal point error. All units verified by biomedical engineering before return to clinical use.
Immediate quarantine and replacement of affected reagent lots. Reflex testing protocol issued. All positive results from affected lots to be re-confirmed. New primer design covering BA.2.86 released.
Software recall: mandatory update to version 2.2.0 resolving FiO2 calibration error. Biomedical engineers visited all affected units within 14 days. Field service documentation required.
A FSCA involving the return of a device to the manufacturer.
A field safety corrective action that has a closure date, inferred by SWRL rule
Medical Device Regulation EU 2017/745 applicable to general medical devices.
Peripheral artery drug-coated angioplasty balloon
A device intended to be used on one individual during a single procedure.
Urgent safety notice regarding MRI scanning protocol. Updated MRI-conditional guidelines issued. Mandatory reprogramming required before any MRI scan. Programmer software update v3.2 released.

Updated IFU regarding post-operative infection prophylaxis protocol. Extended topical antibiotic course (14 days) mandatory post-insertion. Healthcare professionals notified via direct mail and implanting centre visits.
Customer field notification regarding biotin interference in TSH assay. Updated sample preparation protocol and interference screening algorithm released via software update 4.1.2.
A FSCA involving a notice sent to device users with corrective instructions.
Battery end-of-life earlier than specified, risking therapy interruption
Complete loss of electrical function leading to device shutdown
Structural failure of mechanical components under physiological load
Unintended displacement of implant from intended anatomical position
Cardiac lead displacement requiring repositioning or replacement
Risk type related to device malfunction or functional failure.
MDR risk class III - highest risk.
IVD test returns positive result in absence of target condition
IVD test fails to detect target condition in affected individual
Reagent reacts with non-target analyte producing incorrect result
Risk type related to incorrect or misleading diagnostic results.
Sustained-release fluocinolone acetonide ocular implant
Active cochlear implant for sensorineural hearing loss
Cardiac resynchronization therapy with defibrillation
Leadless cardiac pacemaker for atrioventricular synchrony
Cardiac resynchronization therapy lead
An active device designed to be totally introduced into the human body.
A country where a manufacturer is registered or where an incident has been reported.
Reagent lot contamination leading to systematic diagnostic errors
Risk type related to reagent or device contamination.
Thrombus formation on device surface causing embolic events
Mechanical destruction of red blood cells by pump mechanism
Risk type related to physical injury to the patient.
IVD test returns positive result in absence of target condition
IVD test fails to detect target condition in affected individual
Reagent reacts with non-target analyte producing incorrect result
Risk category related to incorrect diagnostic results.
Ceramic-on-ceramic total hip replacement
Transcatheter aortic valve replacement (TAVI)
Transcatheter mitral valve replacement system
Total knee arthroplasty implant system
A device intended to be totally or partially introduced into the human body.
A potential hazard associated with a medical device as classified by the IMDRF nomenclature.
Metallic implant causing MRI image artifacts compromising diagnostics
External EMI affecting pacemaker/ICD sensing and pacing functions
Unintended displacement of implant from intended anatomical position
Cardiac lead displacement requiring repositioning or replacement
Risk category related to suboptimal device performance.
A manufacturer that holds an EU authorization number, inferred by SWRL rule
Device-related infection at implantation site or systemic sepsis
Bacterial biofilm accumulation on implant surface resistant to antibiotics
Risk type related to device-associated infection or biofilm.
Portable patient monitor for vital signs
Large volume infusion pump with drug library
ICU mechanical ventilator with pressure and volume modes
Handheld point-of-care ultrasound probe
High-volume online hemodiafiltration machine
Diagnostic ultrasound imaging system
Magnetic resonance imaging system 3T
A device whose operation depends on a source of energy.
Safety communication to implanting cardiologists regarding optimal fixation technique for ACUITY X4 lead. Enhanced fluoroscopy verification protocol at implantation.
Safety communication regarding outflow graft kinking risk. Updated surgical technique for graft routing issued. Mandatory follow-up CT at 6 months for patients with affected lot numbers.
Safety alert issued to all implanting centres regarding anticoagulation protocol update for HeartMate 3 to reduce device thrombosis risk. INR target range revised to 2.0–3.0.
A FSCA involving a safety communication to users and patients.
The date on which the incident was first detected.
Relates a medical device to its applicable regulatory framework.
Indicates whether the device is implantable.
The regulatory contact email address of the manufacturer.
The number or description of devices affected by the corrective action.
The age of the patient involved in the incident.
Relates an incident to the identified risk according to IMDRF nomenclature.
The sex of the patient involved in the incident.
Relates an incident to the medical device involved.
Relates a manufacturer to the competent authority that supervises it.
The regulatory authorization number issued to the manufacturer.
The unique identifier of the medical device.
The unique identifier of the incident.
The date on which the incident was reported to the competent authority.
Relates a field safety corrective action to the incident that triggered it.
Indicates whether the device is intended for reuse.
The Unique Device Identifier - Device Identifier.
The official name of the manufacturer.
Indicates whether a corrective action was taken in response to the incident.
Relates an incident to the manufacturer that reported it.
Relates a medical device to its regulatory risk class.
Indicates whether the device requires an energy source to operate.
Relates a risk to the type of medical device it affects.
The commercial name of the medical device.
The current status of the field safety corrective action (Open or Closed).
Relates a field safety corrective action to a country where it applies.
Relates an incident to the country where it was reported.
The description of devices or lots within the scope of the corrective action.
The unique identifier of the risk.
The name of the risk according to IMDRF nomenclature.
The severity level of the risk (Critical, Serious, Moderate).
The IMDRF nomenclature code for the risk.
Relates a medical device to its manufacturer.
The unique identifier of the field safety corrective action.
Relates a manufacturer to the country where it is registered.
The Single Registration Number assigned by EUDAMED to the manufacturer.
The EUDAMED reference number for the corrective action.
The name of the country.
The clinical outcome of the incident.
The intended purpose of the medical device as stated by the manufacturer.
A textual description of the resource (risk, incident or corrective action).
The probability of occurrence of the risk (Low, Medium, High).
The date on which the corrective action was closed.
The date on which the corrective action was initiated.
The EUDAMED reference number for the incident.
Indicates whether the device is an in vitro diagnostic device.
The date on which the incident event occurred.
